title: Past simple: write the correct form
lesson: The past simple describes finished actions at a definite past time. Regular verbs add -ed (work → worked), but many common verbs are irregular (go → went, buy → bought). Negatives use did + not + the base verb (didn't like).

items.explanation: Regular verbs add -ed in the past simple: work → worked.

items.text: They {1} to the gym every day.

items.explanation: Past negatives use did + not + base verb: don't like → didn't like.
A consonant + y becomes -ied: study → studied.
A short vowel + single consonant doubles the consonant before -ed: stop → stopped.

items.text: We {1} a great film last night.
She {1} a letter to her friend.

items.explanation: Past negatives use did + not + base verb: didn't know.

items.text: The children {1} in the garden all afternoon.
items.explanation: A vowel + y just adds -ed (no spelling change): play → played.
